Tuesday, April 21, 2026 Meridian State Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation
The Microsoft AI stack at MSDCR
Four layers, one license spine — how the six corrections use cases map to Microsoft's AI platform.
Layer 1 · Productivity
Microsoft 365 Copilot
Outlook · Teams · Word · Excel · PowerPoint · OneDrive — in-flow Copilot for every Case Manager, Classification Officer, Visitation Coordinator, Healthcare Admin, and Reentry Team member.
M365 Copilot UC1–UC6 · daily productivity
Layer 2 · Conversational agents
Copilot Studio
The six MSDCR custom agents — Policy Copilot, Visitation Front Door, Grievance & PRR Router, Reentry Program Match, Records Request Desk, Classification Packet Prep — all built here and grounded in MSDCR SharePoint + policy.
Copilot Studio UC1 · UC2 · UC3 · UC4 · UC5 · UC6
Layer 3 · Apps & workflow
Power Platform
Power Apps (case-management intake forms), Power Automate (grievance + PRR SLA timers, visitation exception routing), Power Pages (public inquiry portal), Dataverse (agent knowledge stores).
Power Platform UC2 · UC3 · UC4 · UC5 · case plumbing
Layer 4 · Advanced AI & governance
Microsoft Foundry
Agent orchestration, model selection, evaluation suites, and governance — the spine for high-stakes use cases. Every Classification Packet Prep recommendation is Foundry-evaluated against policy ground truth; human approval required.
Foundry UC6 classification · governance across all six
Dataverse · shared data spine
The governed tables powering every Power App, Power Automate flow, Power Pages portal, and Copilot Studio agent at MSDCR.
msdcr_residents
18,204 rows · ACA-aligned
msdcr_grievances
1,847 rows · SLA tiered
msdcr_visitor_applications
3,418 rows · family intake
msdcr_program_completions
9,102 rows · reentry grounded

Copilot Studio
Full agent builder
IT
Recent · Last 24 hours
Custom agents · Built for Meridian State in Copilot Studio
Policy Copilot
Grounds answers in the MSDCR Policy Manual (rev 2026R1) + DOP/PREA; flags missing or outdated citations in classification-review packets.
Live · last sync 2 min ago
Visitation Front Door
Routes visitation applications and exceptions from family.msdcr.gov, pre-stages the Coordinator approval loop.
3 new exceptions awaiting review
Grievance & PRR Router
Classifies grievance tier (Emergency / 15-day / 30-day) and triages PRRs by sensitivity into the right queue.
2 SLA risks pending >48h
Reentry Program Match
Scores Reentry Intake Assessments against MCRP program availability and returns ranked placements for the Case Manager.
2 assessments ready to review
Build your own agent
Describe what you want, point it at your SharePoint or OneDrive, and it's ready in a few minutes — no code.
Included with Microsoft 365 Copilot

Word — Release-Planning Letter — Resident J. Patel (DRAFT)
AR
Home Insert Draw Design Layout References Review View Copilot
MERIDIAN STATE DEPT. OF CORRECTIONS AND REHABILITATION
1 MSDCR Headquarters Complex, Meridian, State 00000
Policy & Programs Office · Reentry Coordination
🛡 MSDCR Confidential · PII

Request information (for reference)

Resident: Patel, J. (Resident ID R-2024-018372)
Housing: Complex 2 · Unit 4B · Medium (Level 3) · Scheduled Release: 2026-06-09
Requester: Meridian Community Reentry Partners (MCRP) — Parole District 3
Ref #: MSDCR-R-2026-018372
Letter type: Release-planning letter — program completions + housing plan + parole officer assignment
Requested by MCRP: "Please confirm completed programs (CBT, GED, Welders Guild pre-apprenticeship), approved housing plan, assigned parole officer, and scheduled release date. Please send via the MCRP secure portal (ROI-7 on file)."

Supporting records attached

  • Program completion certificates (CBT 2025-11-22, GED 2026-01-24, Welders Guild pre-apprenticeship 2026-03-30) · Records Division export
  • Approved housing plan on file 2026-04-10 (Pathway House, 12 mi from MCRP District 3)
  • Parole officer assignment confirmation — Ofc. Teresa Lang, MCRP Parole District 3
  • MCRP reception appointment scheduled 2026-06-10 @ 09:00 (confirmed by MCRP portal)
  • Resident consent + signed ROI-7 to MCRP (2026-04-19)

Release-planning letter body will appear here. Click the Copilot logo (top-right) to draft.

Evaluation dimensions · run #2041 · vs baseline run #2040
Groundedness
98.2%▲ 0.4
Citation integrity
100%— 0.0
Policy-criteria coverage
96.1%▲ 1.2
Relevance
94.0%▼ 0.6
Safety (protected-class & bias)
99.6%▲ 0.1
Hallucination rate (lower is better)
0.3%▼ 0.1
View full trace in Foundry ↗ · 214 historical packets in eval suite · Azure OpenAI GPT-4.1 · GCC High
Agent draft (Packet #4 of 7)
Recommend classification tier: Medium (level 3). Criteria met: no major disciplinary in 12 months, program completion (CBT + GED), consistent housing assignment. Reference: DOP 402 §3.2.1.
Foundry evaluator
Policy-ground match 99.1%. Citation DOP 402 §3.2.1 verified against source. No hallucinated criteria. Routing to human approval.
Agent draft (Packet #5 of 7)
Recommend classification tier: Close custody. Criteria: recent disciplinary (fighting, 3/18), incomplete program placement.
Foundry evaluator
Policy-ground match 94.8%. Potential policy conflict with DOP 402 §4.1 (single-incident escalation threshold). Flagged for supervisor review.
Classification Officer approval gate
Awaiting human sign-off. No packet is submitted to the hearing without officer approval.
Foundry evaluation run · Classification Packet Prep
Weekly eval vs. MSDCR Policy Manual · human approval gate on every packet

Agent under evaluation

Classification Packet Prep · UC6 · Copilot Studio agent with Foundry orchestration + evaluation spine.

Evaluation suite

Policy-ground truth match: 98.2% across 214 historical packets. Citations verified against DOP 402 / DOP 906 / PREA standards. Hallucination rate: 0.3% (auto-flagged for human review).

Guardrails

No packet is auto-submitted. Every classification recommendation enters the Classification Officer approval queue. Packets below 95% policy-ground-match are routed for supervisor review.

This week

  • 42 packets drafted · 42 reviewed by an officer · 40 approved, 2 revised
  • Median officer review time: 7 minutes (down from 38 min pre-agent)
  • Foundry evaluation: 98.2% policy-ground match · audit log in Purview
  • Next evaluation run: Friday 5 AM (weekly)
↻
Without Copilot: 38 min packet prep With Classification Packet Prep + Foundry eval + officer approval: 7 min review · saved 31 min per packet

Copilot Pages — Q2 Operations Review
AR
JP
MR
+2
AR
M. Johnson
P. Raman
Live page · 5 editors

Q2 Operations Operations Review

A shared canvas for Policy & Programs. Copilot keeps the numbers and narrative in sync with the source files — when the CorrectionsCore dashboard updates, this page does too.

🛡 Confidential · Internal
✨ Generated by Copilot from Q2 Ops Metrics.xlsx · synced 3 min ago
1,247
cases ▲7%
612
Visitation Front Door in Teams ▲4×
68%
Self-service
4.6/5
Staff CSAT ▲0.3

The story this quarter

public inquiry volume is up 7%, but Coordinator touch-time is down sharply — the Visitation Front Door agent in Teams auto-handled 612 exception requests that would have been Coordinator tickets last quarter, and Policy Copilot surfaced 237 policy-citation exceptions (14 missing classification review references, 62 outdated DOP citations) that are now 82% remediated. Staff CSAT up 0.3 to 4.6/5. The pattern worth watching: records-request turnaround. We processed 184 PRRs but 11 took >2 days — the Records Request Desk rollout in Phase 2 should close that.

✨ Copilot suggestion based on this page + your ops review deck
Want me to add a Top 3 Wins section pulled from the Q2 ops review deck? I'll mirror the deck so if it changes, this page updates.

Copilot summary
Grievance cycle time dropped from 4.2 days to 0.9 days after the Grievance & PRR Router went live — driven by auto-SLA tiering and structured intake. Classification turnaround fell from 38 min to 7 min with Classification Packet Prep — every packet still requires officer approval (Foundry eval 98.2%). Visitation scheduling efficiency up 14%, and the reentry program enrollment climbed 18% QoQ as Program Match surfaces more eligible candidates to case managers.

What's new in this view: cross-vendor agent inventory. Your Microsoft agents, partner agents (CorrectionsCore, Salesforce, ServiceNow), and in-house Azure-OpenAI custom agents all appear alongside each other — with the same identity, DLP, and Conditional Access controls. Shadow agents surface automatically via Defender telemetry.

Every agent has its own non-human identity, governed like a service principal

Verified by Microsoft Entra External ID. ID is masked in the record view.

Resident lookup reads from Dataverse; eligibility window is enforced by policy.

60 minutes

MSDCR Records Office›Public Records›New Request #MSDCR-P-2026-0411007
Records Request Desk will pre-scan for PII & CJIS

Requester details

Identity verified via Microsoft Entra External ID (one-time verification for public portal).
Journalist

Request scope

Scope defines the fee estimate, redaction surface, and 10-day statutory clock.
Incident reports

Redaction pre-screen

Administrative pre-scan only; Legal Office performs statutory review before release.
Records Request Desk · pre-screen
Will pre-scan for resident PII, medical PII, and CJIS-protected data. Legal review occurs before release. Nothing is released by the agent; the agent never writes out public records.
Copilot Studio Administrative only

Delivery method

Secure portal is default for journalism & legal requesters.
Encrypted email Secure portal download Physical mail

Statutory clock

Auto-extension available with written notice per MSDCR 9.01.
Case #MSDCR-P-2026-0411007 · Filed 2026-04-11 · Day 6 of 10 statutory days · Auto-extend available with written notice
Request routing Dataverse
New record · MSDCR-P-…1007 Assigned: A. Okoye · Records Coordinator Est. page volume: 420 (below fee threshold) Prior requests by requester: 3 (all fulfilled)

  • 3 matches: Resident PII likely in narrative fields
  • 1 match: CJIS criminal history reference
  • No medical PII detected in Q1 dataset

CJIS 5.9 — Criminal history limits
Routes to: A. Okoye

Resident context

Pulled from CorrectionsCore; housing and parole officer assignments are required before coordinator review.
Pending
Pending status blocks coordinator approval step; Program Match will propose housing.
Ofc. Teresa Lang · MCRP District 3

Needs assessment

Coordinator-reviewed. Needs drive Program Match scoring.
Housing NoneModerateHigh Employment readiness NoneModerateHigh Mental health NoneModerateHigh Substance-use support NoneModerateHigh Education / GED NoneModerateHigh Family reunification support NoneModerateHigh Transportation NoneModerateHigh ID replacement NoneModerateHigh

Program match suggestions

Ranked by fit score; final enrollment requires coordinator approval.
Reentry Program Match · top suggestions
1 · Housing — Pathway House (2 open beds, 12 mi from MCRP)
2 · Employment — Welders Guild apprenticeship (cohort May 12, 94% completion)
3 · MH — Group CBT weekly, Thursdays
Copilot Studio Foundry eval 94.1% placement accuracy

Risk / protective factors

Static only — officer confirms. Agent cannot modify risk flags.
Protective: Family engagement — yes (weekly visits confirmed)
Risk: Prior SUD history — yes (meets Pathway House eligibility)
Housing stability (pre-incarceration): Moderate (12 mo stable, then lapse)

Hallucination rate
100%
Citation-verified
Packets below 95% policy-ground match route for supervisor review before officer approval.
Packet #7 of 7 on Thursday's docket

Approval gate

Human sign-off is a hard gate — no packet reaches the hearing docket without it.
No packet is submitted to hearing without officer approval
The agent can assemble and evaluate. Only the named Classification Officer can route to the hearing docket.

Warden Review Queue›Visitation Exceptions›Patel, J. #MSDCR-X-2026-044219
Front Door pre-triaged · Coordinator concurs

Exception context

Summary of the request and the applicable policy rule the exception is against.
Request summary
Family member Jessica Huang requests video-visit eligibility for resident J. Patel following his 2026-04-19 medical admin flag. Standard rule: 30-day post-medical cooldown. Exception reason cited: “pre-release reintegration plan calls for increased family contact per Reentry Program Match.”

Agent assessment

Policy-grounded reasoning · cross-checked against the resident's reentry plan.
Visitation Front Door · assessment
Eligible for video visit under policy 4.07 exception clause (medical cooldown waiver for pre-release). Cross-check with Reentry Program Match: YES · active pre-release reintegration plan on file. Recommendation: approve with 15-minute limit.
Copilot Studio Cross-agent check: Reentry Program Match Policy-ground 97.4%

Supporting documents

Click-through (read-only) for Warden reference during decision.
Policy 4.07 — medical cooldown exceptions
Reentry plan #MSDCR-R-2026-018372
Prior visit history · 12 video visits, 0 incidents

Warden decision

Your decision is final and logs to the applicant's portal record in real time.

  • Geography: US Gov Cloud (GCC High)

Corrections & Rehabilitation Workflow Automation
OFFICIAL USE · AUDIT LOGGED
Every run captured to Microsoft Purview · GCC

Grievance SLA Escalation

Triggered by new records in Dataverse · msdcr_grievances. Grounds routing in the Grievance & PRR Router agent; escalates to Warden + Purview audit on SLA risk.

⚡
Trigger
When a grievance is submitted
Dataverse · msdcr_grievances · On create
◆
Action · Copilot Studio
Classify by tier
Call Grievance & PRR Router agent · policy-grounded classification
Tier: Emergency · 15-day · 30-day
?
Condition
Tier equals "Emergency"?
Branches: Yes / No
Yes
✉
Action
Notify Warden + Deputy Director
Teams · urgent-card · Outlook · high-importance
No
📋
Action
Set SLA deadline + Planner task
Planner · MSDCR Grievance Board · due = now + tier days
⏱
Delay until
SLA deadline minus 2 days
Resume execution 48 hours before SLA
🔍
Action · Dataverse
Check: is grievance resolved?
Dataverse query · msdcr_grievances.status == "Resolved"
!
Action · If not resolved
Escalate to Warden + Purview audit
Teams alert + log audit event (audit.msdcr.grievance.sla_risk) in Microsoft Purview

PRR Intake Router

Pre-scans inbound public records requests for PII / CJIS / protected-class content, routes them, and starts the statutory 10-day clock. Grounded by the Records Request Desk agent.

⚡
Trigger
When a new PRR is submitted
Dataverse · msdcr_public_records · On create
◆
Action · Copilot Studio
Pre-scan for PII / CJIS / protected-class data
Call Records Request Desk agent · sensitivity classification
Flags: protected_class · pii · cjis
?
Condition
Contains protected-class data?
Branches: Yes / No
Yes
⚖
Action
Route to Legal Review queue
Planner · MSDCR Legal · notify General Counsel via Teams
No
⏱
Action
Route to Records Office · start 10-day clock
Dataverse update · msdcr_public_records.sla_start = utcNow()
🌐
Action · Power Pages
Publish public status page entry
family.msdcr.gov/records-request/{id} · case # + ETA
✉
Action · Office 365 Outlook
Email acknowledgement to requester
Template · "Your PRR has been received" · includes case # + status link

Visitation Exception Routing

Triggered by exception submissions from family.msdcr.gov. Grounds eligibility in the Visitation Front Door agent, cross-checks active reentry plans, and routes to the right approver.

⚡
Trigger
When a visitation exception is submitted
Dataverse · msdcr_visitation_exceptions · On create
◆
Action · Copilot Studio
Assess eligibility
Call Visitation Front Door agent · policy-grounded assessment
Recommendation · confidence score
◆
Action · Copilot Studio
Cross-check active reentry plan
Call Reentry Program Match agent · conflict check
?
Condition
Confidence ≥ 90% AND no policy conflict?
Branches: Yes / No
Yes
📋
Action
Route to Visitation Coordinator queue
Pre-filled recommendation · one-click approve
No
!
Action
Escalate to Warden with full context
Teams adaptive card · policy citations attached
🌐
Action · Power Pages
Update family.msdcr.gov status page
Applicant sees "Under review" with ETA
🔏
Action · Microsoft Purview
Log decision + rationale to audit
audit.msdcr.visitation.exception_routed · immutable 180-day retention

Classification Packet Approval Notifier

Runs after a classification packet passes Foundry evaluation. Sends the assigned Classification Officer a Teams adaptive card and escorts the packet through the Thursday docket.

⚡
Trigger
When a packet passes Foundry eval
Dataverse · msdcr_classification_reviews · status = "evaluated"
🔍
Action · CorrectionsCore
Look up assigned Classification Officer
CorrectionsCore.Roster.Read · by facility + shift
📨
Action · Microsoft Teams
Send adaptive card to officer
"{n} packets ready for Thursday's docket" · Approve / Request changes buttons
⏱
Delay until
24 hours before docket OR officer acts
Whichever comes first
?
Condition
Officer approved?
Branches: Yes / No
Yes
📋
Action
Add to hearing docket + Purview audit
CorrectionsCore.Docket.Write · audit.msdcr.classif.approved
No
↻
Action
Return to Classification Packet Prep agent
Include officer revision notes · re-queue for next eval cycle
📊
Action · Power BI
Update operations dashboard
Dataset refresh · MSDCR Ops · classification-docket tile

Reentry Program Match Notifier

Runs program-match scoring after a Reentry Intake Assessment is submitted. Queries MCRP partner availability and drops ranked recommendations into the Case Manager's queue.

⚡
Trigger
When a Reentry Intake Assessment is submitted
Dataverse · msdcr_reentry_assessments · On create
◆
Action · Copilot Studio
Run program-match scoring
Call Reentry Program Match agent · ranked list with rationale
Top 5 programs · confidence & fit scores
🔌
Action · Custom connector
Query MCRP partner availability
Meridian Community Reentry Partners · beds/slots/waitlist
✉
Action · Office 365 Outlook
Email ranked recommendations
To Case Manager + MCRP coordinator · HTML summary + case link
📋
Action · Microsoft Planner
Create Planner tasks per approved placement
One task per program · assigned to Case Manager
📄
Action · Office 365 Outlook + Word
Schedule release-planning letter draft
Outlook meeting with Word template attached · 2 weeks pre-release

Records Request Triage

First-touch classifier for records requests arriving through the public Power Pages portal. Splits medical records admin from general PRR and legal discovery before the SLA clock starts.

⚡
Trigger
When a records request is received
Power Pages form · family.msdcr.gov/records-request · On submit
◆
Action · Copilot Studio
Classify request type
Call Records Request Desk agent · Medical records admin / General PRR / Legal discovery
Type · requester role · urgency
?
Condition
Type = Medical records admin?
Branches: Yes / No
Yes
⚕
Action
Route to Healthcare Admin queue
HIPAA banner attached · BAA-scoped access only
No
→
Action
Hand off to PRR Intake Router flow
Invokes child flow · carries correlation id
📨
Action · Microsoft Teams
Notify triage coordinator
Channel post · @mention · includes request summary
💾
Action · Dataverse
Update case status
msdcr_public_records.status = "triaged" · set route_queue

I-30 Grievance Response Timer

Scheduled daily sweep of I-30 (informal 30-day) grievances approaching SLA. Escalates to Warden + Ombudsman when >95% of SLA has elapsed; otherwise nudges the assigned Coordinator.

⏰
Trigger · Scheduled
Daily at 7:00 AM (America/Denver)
Recurrence · every 1 day
🔍
Action · Dataverse
Query open I-30 grievances approaching SLA
msdcr_grievances · tier = "I-30" · due within 3 days
Returns: list of cases + hours remaining
◆
Action · Copilot Studio
Classify urgency + remaining time
Call Grievance & PRR Router agent · computes % SLA elapsed
?
Condition
Any > 95% of SLA elapsed?
Branches: Yes / No
Yes
!
Action
Escalate to Warden + Ombudsman
Teams urgent-card + Outlook high-importance email
No
📨
Action
Reminder to assigned Coordinator
Teams channel message · includes case links + hours left
📊
Action · Power BI
Update grievance cycle-time dashboard
Dataset refresh · MSDCR Grievance Ops · I-30 tile

Visitation Schedule Publisher

Weekly publisher that pulls approved visits, generates per-unit schedule grids, and pushes them to the public portal plus Outlook calendar invites for approved visitors.

⏰
Trigger · Scheduled
Every Sunday at 6:00 AM
Recurrence · weekly · America/Denver
🔍
Action · Dataverse
Pull approved visits for upcoming week
msdcr_visitor_applications · status = "approved" · visit_date within next 7 days
📐
Action · Power Apps canvas
Generate per-unit schedule grids
Visitation Scheduler canvas app · 6 facility grids
🌐
Action · Power Pages
Publish to portal
family.msdcr.gov/visits/schedule-by-unit · auto-refreshes cache
📅
Action · Office 365 Outlook
Send calendar invites to approved visitors
One invite per approved visit · includes check-in instructions
📨
Action · Microsoft Teams
Notify Coordinator + Complex Administrators
Channel post with per-unit summary counts
🔏
Action · Microsoft Purview
Log publication event to audit
audit.msdcr.visitation.schedule_published · weekly retention

Ask Meridian · popular questions
Can my child visit? How do video visits work? What ID do I need? Can I deposit money online? When is my loved one eligible for reentry programs?

FAQ · Visits · 21 answers

Minor children, ID requirements, dress code, video kiosks.

FAQ · Money & mail · 14 answers

Deposits, commissary, approved vendors, mail address formats.

Visits

Schedule a visit, check eligibility, or look up status. Warden-approved window: Fri–Sun 9 AM – 4 PM.

Home › Visits

Schedule a new visit

In-person, video, or non-contact. Check eligibility in two minutes with our virtual assistant.

Video visit kiosks

Our video kiosks run on the family.msdcr.gov portal, no app install. Use Chrome, Edge, or Safari.

Kiosk guide →

Register as an approved visitor

Background check required. Family and legal representatives complete the Entra External ID flow.

Register →
Your upcoming visits
Sat Apr 2610:30–11:30 AM MST
Video visit · Complex 2, Unit 4BResident J. Patel · Approved
Eligibility check
Sat May 102:00–3:00 PM MST
In-person · Complex 2 · Visitor CenterResident J. Patel · Held
Pending Warden
Visit rules at a glance
  • Acceptable ID: State-issued driver's license, state ID, tribal ID, passport, or military ID.
  • Minor children: Must be accompanied by a legal parent or guardian. Birth certificate or court order required.
  • Dress code: No solid orange, no gang-affiliated insignia, no revealing attire. Full dress code: MSDCR DOP 507.
  • Items allowed: One clear bag, keys, ID, $20 in small bills. Lockers at the visitor center.
  • Duration: In-person 60 min · video 30 min. Extensions require warden approval via the exception process.

Public records

File a public-records request or look up the status of an existing one. Routine requests are answered within ten business days.

Home › Records

File a new request

Describe what records you need and the date range. Our Records Request Desk agent pre-screens for PII and protected-class data.

Begin →

Look up a request

Enter your case reference or request ID to track status, estimated fees, and delivery method.

Look up →

Fee schedule

First 30 pages free. Copies $0.10/page, electronic release free. Legal-review fees apply to cases with protected-class data.

Schedule →
Your recent requests
#MSDCR-P-2026-0411007Filed Apr 11 · Routine
Q1 use-of-force incident reports · Complex 4Records Office · Daniela Martinez
Day 7 of 10
What you can and cannot request
  • Available: Incident reports, visitation logs, contracts, policies (DOP series), staff rosters, PREA audit summaries.
  • Redacted or restricted: Resident medical records (HIPAA), CJIS-protected data, victim information, ongoing investigations.
  • Not available through PRR: Individual resident case files — contact the resident's legal representative.
  • Appeal: If a request is partially denied, you may appeal to the MSDCR Office of General Counsel within 30 days.

Grievances

File or follow up on a grievance. Residents and family advocates can submit a secure intake — every grievance is tracked to resolution with a Purview audit trail.

Home › Grievances

File a new grievance

Safety, medical, property, staff conduct, housing, discrimination, or other. Tiered SLA (Emergency / 15-day / 30-day).

File →

Check status

Enter your case # or sign in to see every grievance you've filed and its current tier, coordinator, and SLA clock.

Check →

Ombudsman

If your grievance is not resolved within the SLA, you may escalate to the MSDCR Ombudsman's Office.

Contact ombudsman →
Open cases filed by you
#MSDCR-G-2026-044210Filed Apr 19 · 30-day tier
Missed medical appointment · Complex 3, Unit 7ACoordinator: Unit 7A · Ombudsman cc
Day 3 of 30
How grievances are handled
  • Emergency tier (safety risk) — 24-hour response, Warden notified immediately via Teams adaptive card.
  • 15-day tier (medical, PREA, ADA) — assigned to unit coordinator + specialist ombudsman.
  • 30-day tier (standard) — assigned to unit coordinator; Grievance & PRR Router classifies automatically.
  • Appeals — submit within 10 days of decision to the Office of General Counsel.
  • Retaliation protection — every grievance is audit-logged to Microsoft Purview with read-only access for Office of General Counsel.

Frequently asked questions

128 answers · Updated 2 days ago · Powered by the Visitation Front Door agent

Home › FAQ
Visits (21) Money & mail (14) Reentry (19) ID & docs (12) Legal rights (18) Grievances (15) Records (9) Medical admin (20)
Can my minor child attend a visit?

Yes. A legal parent or guardian must accompany the child. Bring a birth certificate or court order. Children under 8 may not visit high-security units.

What ID do I need to visit?

State-issued driver's license, state ID, tribal ID, passport, or military ID. Expired IDs are not accepted. Legal representatives must also present bar credentials.

How do video visits work?

Video visits run from family.msdcr.gov. Use Chrome, Edge, or Safari on a device with a camera. No app install required. A family member must be registered and verified via Entra External ID.

Can I deposit money online?

Yes. Deposits are handled through our approved vendor. Daily limit $300, monthly limit $1,500. See Money & mail for full terms.

What does "30-day tier" mean on my grievance?

Grievance & PRR Router agent classifies every grievance into one of three tiers. 30-day tier means a coordinator will respond within 30 days. Emergency tier gets a 24-hour response.

How do I request a public record?

Use the Records page. Routine requests answered within 10 business days. First 30 pages free.

My loved one is approaching release. What do I do next?

See Reentry Resources. MCRP (Meridian Community Reentry Partners) will contact you 60 days before release to coordinate housing, ID replacement, and parole-officer assignment.

Why was my request flagged for legal review?

Some records contain protected-class data (medical, CJIS, victim info). Our Records Request Desk agent pre-screens for this, and legal review ensures release is compliant before delivery.

Browse all 128 answers → Or

Reentry resources

Housing, employment, ID replacement, mental health, and parole — what your loved one's reentry plan may include.

Home › Reentry

Housing & transitional living

MCRP partners provide transitional housing including Pathway House, Riverton Transitional, and Northside Sober Living.

See partners →

Employment & apprenticeships

Welders Guild pre-apprenticeship, CDL training, culinary bridge programs. 94% completion for returning students.

Programs →

ID replacement

MSDCR coordinates with Meridian State DMV for pre-release ID. Start 90 days before projected release date.

Start →

Parole officer finder

Look up the assigned parole officer by district, or call MCRP (555) 204-1001 for coordination.

Find officer →
MCRP partner directory — Meridian Community Reentry Partners
  • District 1 · Meridian City — 221 Bridge St · Supervisor: Ofc. R. Chavez · (555) 301-1001
  • District 2 · Northridge — 40 Harbor Lane · Supervisor: Ofc. M. Daniels · (555) 301-1002
  • District 3 · Eastlake — 88 Pathway Ave · Supervisor: Ofc. T. Lang · (555) 301-1003
  • District 4 · Riverton — 12 Mill Rd · Supervisor: Ofc. S. Patel · (555) 301-1004

Records Office(555) 204-0930
General Counsel(555) 204-0955
Tip line (non-emergency)(555) 204-0955

Portal familiar · en Español

Programe visitas, solicite registros públicos y siga una queja. Información también disponible en Vietnamita y Dari.

Inicio › Español

Programar una visita

Video, en persona o sin contacto. Verifique su elegibilidad en dos minutos con nuestro asistente virtual.

Solicitar un registro público

Presente una solicitud y siga su estado. Las solicitudes rutinarias se responden en diez días hábiles.

Comenzar →

Presentar una queja

Admisión segura para residentes y defensores familiares. Cada queja se rastrea hasta su resolución con un registro de auditoría.

Presentar →
Servicios de idioma
  • Intérprete telefónico sin costo · marque (555) 204-0900 y presione 2.
  • Formularios impresos en español disponibles en cada centro de visitas.
  • Asistente virtual (Visitation Front Door) responde en español · inglés · vietnamita · dari.
  • Letra grande y TTY disponibles bajo solicitud.
Ver en English →

External identity & access
Microsoft Entra External ID · email OTP, vouched, and bar-verified flows
RoleAuthenticationAccess scopeActive usersSign-ins · 30d
Anonymous visitorNonePublic FAQ, facility map, contact form—9,143
Authenticated family memberEntra External ID · email OTPSchedule visits, file grievances on behalf of resident, view status2,8413,112
Resident advocateEntra External ID · vouchedGrievance follow-up, PRR, caseload lookups184227
Legal representativeEntra External ID · bar-verifiedPRR, attorney-client communications channel6388
Authentication flow
  1. Visitor enters email on family.msdcr.gov
  2. Entra External ID sends 6-digit OTP (5-min TTL)
  3. Session-bound tokens; PII field-masking from Dataverse
  4. Audit event posts to Microsoft Purview on every sign-in

Visitation Front Door · conversation outcomes
Self-served · 68%
Scheduled visit · 22%
Escalated to coordinator · 10%
Executive summary · auto-drafted by Copilot

Sessions up 9% MoM, driven by a 14% lift in /schedule-visit traffic after the bilingual Visitation Front Door agent went live April 3. Deflection rate held at 98% with only 10% of chats needing human coordinator escalation. PRR filings via portal grew 31%; SLA compliance remains above 96%. Next focus: /visits/video-kiosk-guide publishes Thursday and should reduce family phone volume further.

MSDCR Corrections Copilot roadmap — 24 weeks
DK

MSDCR Corrections Copilot roadmap · 24 weeks · ~$350K

Three phases aligned to UC1→UC6. Phase 1 lands Policy Copilot (UC1) + Visitation Front Door (UC2). Phase 2 lands Grievance & PRR Router (UC3) + Reentry Program Match (UC4). Phase 3 lands Records Request Desk (UC5) + Classification Packet Prep (UC6) with Microsoft Foundry as the governance spine. Shared with the MSDCR×IT AI operating council.

2,847
MSDCR staff
6
Use cases
$350K
Total budget
3/3
Phases on track
Kickoff
Week 1
Phase 0+1 GA
Week 10
Phase 2 GA
Week 18 · now
Phase 3 GA
Week 24
Phase 1

Staff knowledge + family front door · UC1 & UC2 · Weeks 1–10

✓ Completed · Week 10
Scope

MSDCR×IT AI operating model, Foundry governance baseline, policy manual + director-memo ingestion, Teams + public-portal entry points. Agents: Policy Copilot (UC1) + Visitation Front Door (UC2).

Use cases live
  • UC1 — Policy Copilot for staff (grounded in MSDCR Policy Manual + SOPs)
  • UC2 — Visitation Front Door (public + family, eligibility + scheduling)
  • Audit log flowing to Purview; Foundry evaluations enabled
Outcomes hit
  • ✓ Policy answers consistent across 3 shifts
  • ✓ 68% of family inquiries auto-served
  • ✓ Governance guardrails GA'd
Investment

$135K total (weeks 1–10)

$75K grant-funding potential

Phase 2

Grievance + reentry flow · UC3 & UC4 · Weeks 10–18

● In progress · GA Week 18
Scope

Grievance & PRR Router (UC3) — resident grievance + public-records request triage with SLA tracking. Reentry Program Match (UC4) — case-manager–facing candidate surfacing for education, treatment, job-readiness programs.

Use cases piloting
  • UC3 — Grievance + PRR intake, tier + SLA assignment
  • UC4 — Program Match candidate lists with case-manager review
  • Power Automate SLA timers + escalation
Outcomes target
  • Grievance cycle time ≤ 1 day
  • PRR SLA compliance ≥ 95%
  • Program enrollment +15% QoQ
Investment

$110K (weeks 10–18)

Funding: TBD

Phase 3

Records Request Desk + Classification Packet Prep · UC5 & UC6 · Weeks 18–24

◷ Planned · GA Week 24 · Foundry spine
Scope

Records Request Desk (UC5) — non-clinical healthcare admin intake, appointment workflow, audit packets. Classification Packet Prep (UC6) — assembles packets, flags policy criteria; every recommendation requires officer approval, Foundry-evaluated against policy ground truth.

Use cases target
  • UC5 — Records Request Desk (admin-only, no clinical)
  • UC6 — Classification Packet Prep · Foundry evaluation
  • Human approval gate on every classification recommendation
  • Quarterly AI governance cadence formalized
Outcomes target
  • Classification turnaround ≤ 10 min · with officer approval
  • Foundry eval ≥ 95% policy-ground match
  • Human-in-the-loop · 100% of UC6 recommendations
Investment

$125K (weeks 18–24)

Funding: TBD

Governance

MSDCR×IT AI council meets biweekly. Purview auto-labels 100% of prompts. Data boundaries staff/resident/community enforced; CJIS applies to resident records. Microsoft Foundry evaluates every Classification Packet Prep recommendation against MSDCR policy ground truth. Every UC6 action requires officer approval before submission.

Risks watched

CorrectionsCore connector decision (Aquera vs direct API), license utilization (rebalance quarterly), Staff CSAT response rate during Staff Pulse Survey migration, Phase 2/3 funding confirmation, ACA accreditation timing (May mock review).

Next check-in

Week 18 Executive readout. Executive Readout Agent is drafting the one-pager now.
